content:
morning. I can smell the yeast on my forearms, a scent that is honest and slow, unlike the sharp, metallic tang of the city’s desperation. Darnell is looking for a rhythm that doesn't exist, chasing a frequency that only leads to exhaustion. He thinks the city is a race; I know it is a mountain.

"You're vibrating, Darnell," I say, my voice low, cutting through the frantic noise of the distant BAR
